summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held its first meeting of 2022, appointing officers, approving the 2022 meeting calendar, and hearing three zoning cases. Case ZC2021-007 approved a preliminary and final development plan for a welding/assembly business at 6600 Patricia Boulevard despite neighborhood opposition. Case ZC2021-008 recommended approval of a 117-lot residential subdivision called Cobblestone on Kerbet Road by Crane/Grand Communities (Fisher Homes), with multiple conditions including a second emergency access point. Case ZC2021-009 approved a zone change from B1 to R3 for parcels at 1918 Main Street to allow consolidation for a single-family dwelling.
decisions: Approved ZC2021-007 PDP/FDP for 6600 Patricia Boulevard with staff-recommended conditions
Recommended approval of ZC2021-008 zone change for 117-lot Cobblestone subdivision at 1867 Kerbet Road with conditions
Recommended approval of ZC2021-009 zone change from B1 to R3 for 1918 Main Street
action_items: Staff (Harry Holder) to work with Grand Communities/Fisher Homes on reworking subdivision plan to provide second emergency ingress/egress access
Grand Communities to complete traffic impact study review with county engineer and ODOT
Grand Communities to provide landscape plan with buffering requirements per Article VI
Grand Communities to make road improvements to Kerbet Road as part of phase one of development
Applicant for 6600 Patricia Boulevard to comply with staff conditions including paving easement, privacy fence, landscaping, and lighting
Applicant for 1918 Main Street to proceed with lot consolidation through Board of Trustees
Staff to recruit zoning commission alternates
key_amounts: Lot sizes ranging from 6,447 to 17,252 square feet in Cobblestone
Home prices ranging from $284,000 to $350,000 in Cobblestone
Home square footages ranging from 1,200 to 2,400 sq ft in Cobblestone
Minimum 1,400 square foot homes required per staff recommendation
0.562 acres consolidated lot at 1918 Main Street
Maximum 4 semi-truck deliveries per month at 6600 Patricia Boulevard
400-600 cars on weekends during ball season on Kerbet Road
notable_topics: PDP/FDP approval for welding and assembly business at 6600 Patricia Boulevard (TPT Holdings - platform tennis court manufacturer)
Cobblestone subdivision - 117-lot residential development on Kerbet Road by Grand Communities/Fisher Homes
Traffic and safety concerns on Kerbet Road including ball field traffic at John Heisler Ballpark
Emergency access and second ingress/egress requirements for new subdivision
Water and sewer infrastructure extension from State Route 132
Zone change from B1 to R3 at 1918 Main Street for residential consolidation
Neighborhood opposition to commercial use on Patricia Boulevard
Road improvement needs on Kerbet Road
attendees: Mark (TPT Holdings owner, applicant for 6600 Patricia Blvd)
Kirk Ritter (Grand Communities, applicant representative for Cobblestone)
John Heisler Ballpark representative (junior sports board member)
Rosie Strict (resident, 6726 Wood Street / 1920 Main Street)
notes: The video title says 1.4.2022 but the transcript states January 3rd, 2022 - likely the actual meeting was January 4, 2022 based on the video title. The property owner at 1867 Kerbet Road (the Cobblestone subdivision site) reportedly passed away the week before the meeting. The traffic impact study for Cobblestone was conducted in November, not during baseball season, which was a concern raised by neighbors. Mr. Frisbee was absent from roll call. The 6600 Patricia Boulevard PBDD zone change had been previously approved in August 2021 under the 2008 zoning resolution; only the development plan was being considered at this meeting. Multiple residents from both Patricia Boulevard and Kerbet Road areas expressed strong opposition to the respective developments.
title: Zoning Commission Mtg 1.4.2022
